activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: Dark clouds are moving over the park and the wind is getting stronger.

options.text: It's going to rain.
It going to rain.
The weather is going to get worse.
The weather will getting worse.

activities.explanation: Las nubes y el viento son indicios presentes. «It's going to rain» y «is going to get worse» están bien construidas.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: A glass is balanced on the edge of the table and the cat is walking towards it.

options.text: The cat is going knock it.
The glass is going to fall.
Nothing going to happen.
The cat is going to knock the glass over.

activities.explanation: La posición del vaso y el movimiento del gato permiten predecir «is going to fall» y «is going to knock ... over».

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The fuel gauge is on empty and the nearest petrol station is twenty kilometres away.

options.text: We're going to run out of fuel.
The car is going to stop soon.
We going to reach the station easily.
The car going run forever.

activities.explanation: El indicador vacío es evidencia directa de que se agotará el combustible y el coche se detendrá.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: Leo has not slept all night and can hardly keep his eyes open.

options.text: Leo is going fall asleep.
Leo going to stay energetic.
He's going to fall asleep soon.
He's going to find it hard to concentrate.

activities.explanation: Su cansancio visible justifica ambas predicciones; las dos usan «is going to + infinitivo».

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The cake is black on top and smoke is coming from the oven.

options.text: The cake is going to taste burnt.
The smoke is going disappear yesterday.
The cake going to be perfect.
The smoke alarm is going to go off.

activities.explanation: El color y el humo son señales presentes; permiten prever el sabor quemado y la activación de la alarma.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: A cyclist is approaching a deep pothole without looking at the road.

options.text: The cyclist will to avoid everything.
The cyclist is going to hit the pothole.
They are going to lose their balance.
They going to safely passed it.

activities.explanation: La trayectoria visible permite anticipar el golpe y la pérdida de equilibrio; ambas formas son gramaticales.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The queue reaches around the block and only one ticket desk is open.

options.text: We're going to wait for a long time.
The queue is going to move slowly.
We going wait no time.
The desk going to serving everyone instantly.

activities.explanation: La longitud de la cola y la única taquilla son evidencia de una espera larga y un avance lento.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: Mina has practised the presentation every day and now speaks without notes.

options.text: Mina going to forget every word.
She is going give a confident presentation.
She's going to do well.
Her preparation is going to help her.

activities.explanation: La práctica y la fluidez actuales son indicios de un buen resultado y de que la preparación será útil.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The river is already above the warning line and heavy rain is continuing upstream.

options.text: The river is going to rise further.
Some low fields are going to flood.
The water going to vanish immediately.
The rain is going stopped last week.

activities.explanation: El nivel actual y la lluvia continua justifican las predicciones de subida y posibles inundaciones.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The laptop fan is extremely loud and the case feels unusually hot.

options.text: The laptop going work cooler yesterday.
It is going to overheat.
The heat is going to cause a problem.
It going to becoming cold.

activities.explanation: El ruido y la temperatura son indicios observables de sobrecalentamiento y de un problema próximo.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The restaurant has no free tables and several groups are waiting outside.

options.text: We are going to need a reservation.
It going to be completely empty.
We going wait never.
It's going to be difficult to get a table.

activities.explanation: La ausencia de mesas y los grupos esperando permiten prever la necesidad de reserva y la dificultad para entrar.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The paint is still wet and a child is reaching towards the wall.

options.text: The child going to keep perfectly clean.
The child is going to touch the paint.
Their hand is going to get covered in paint.
Their hand will getting cleaner.

activities.explanation: El movimiento de la mano hacia la pintura húmeda hace previsibles el contacto y la mancha.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The home team is leading 4–0 with only two minutes left.

options.text: The home team is going to win.
The visitors are going to need a remarkable comeback.
The home team going lose four goals yesterday.
The match is going continue for three days.

activities.explanation: El marcador y el tiempo restante son evidencia clara de una victoria probable y de la dificultad del rival.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The cupboard door is loose and one hinge has already come away.

options.text: The door is going to fall off.
The hinge going repair itself.
The cupboard is going to need a repair.

activities.explanation: La bisagra desprendida permite prever que la puerta caerá y que hará falta una reparación.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: Sales have fallen for six months and the shop has put everything at half price.

options.text: The shop going expand yesterday.
The shop is going to close.
The business is going to make changes.

activities.explanation: La caída sostenida y la liquidación son indicios de cierre o de cambios importantes en el negocio.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The temperature has dropped below zero and water is lying across the untreated road.

options.text: The road going remain warm.
The road is going to become icy.
Driving conditions are going to get dangerous.

activities.explanation: El agua y la temperatura bajo cero son evidencia de hielo próximo y peores condiciones de conducción.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The speaker has lost her voice and can only whisper a few words.

options.text: She isn't going to deliver the talk normally.

activities.explanation: La pérdida de voz hace previsibles la imposibilidad de hablar con normalidad y la necesidad de ayuda.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: Several tiles are missing from the roof and another storm is approaching.

options.text: Rain is going to get into the house.
The roof going repair itself before lunch.
The damage is going to get worse.

activities.explanation: Los huecos y la tormenta próxima permiten anticipar entrada de agua y más daños.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The small meeting room is booked for twenty people, but forty have accepted the invitation.

options.text: Everyone going fit comfortably.

activities.explanation: La diferencia entre capacidad y asistentes es evidencia de hacinamiento y de la necesidad de cambiar de sala.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The phone battery is at one per cent and there is no charger nearby.

options.text: The phone is going to switch off.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The candidate answers every question clearly and the interviewers keep smiling and taking positive notes.

options.text: The candidate is going to make a strong impression.
The interview going fail before it started.

activities.explanation: Las respuestas claras y las reacciones positivas son indicios de una buena impresión y consideración seria.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: Cracks have appeared around the pipe and water is dripping through the ceiling below.

options.text: The leak is going to cause more damage.
The ceiling is going to need repairing.

activities.explanation: Las grietas y el goteo son evidencia de daños futuros y de la necesidad de reparar el techo.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The research team has repeated the experiment five times and obtained the same result each time.

options.text: The team going ignore every result yesterday.
The findings are going to support their conclusion.

activities.explanation: La repetición consistente es evidencia para apoyar una conclusión e informar de un patrón estable.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The path is covered in wet leaves and two walkers have already slipped.

options.text: Someone else is going to slip if they are not careful.
The path going become completely dry in the rain.

activities.explanation: Las hojas mojadas y las caídas previas justifican tanto el riesgo de otra caída como la peligrosidad del camino.

activities.instructions: Select both sentences that correctly describe the evidence.
activities.prompt: The server is responding more slowly each minute and error messages are appearing for many users.

options.text: The service is going to become unavailable.
